evals.prompt: Set up a new automation script called 's3-export' — it reads records, batches them, and writes an export file. It needs AWS access via a profile.
evals.expected_output: Ran /starting-work first; scaffolded scripts/s3-export/ with package.json (@m3l-automation/s3-export, workspace:* dep), tsconfig.json extending tsconfig.base.json, and a modular src/ skeleton: thin main.ts composition root, config.ts declaring M3LConfigParameter set (including an AWS_PROFILE_PARAM_NAME parameter) with M3LConfigValidators, hooks.ts, and a steps/run-export.ts starter taking injected deps. Added the root tsconfig.json reference, ran pnpm install + pnpm build + the smoke run, then handed off implementation.
expectations: Runs /starting-work before writing any scripts/*/src path
Creates scripts/s3-export/package.json naming @m3l-automation/s3-export with a workspace:* dependency on @m3l-automation/m3l-common
Creates scripts/s3-export/tsconfig.json extending ../../tsconfig.base.json with a reference to packages/m3l-common/tsconfig.build.json
main.ts is a thin composition root that constructs Core.M3LScript and calls script.run — no business logic
Business logic lives in a steps/ module that takes its dependencies as parameters (not module-level state, not process.env)
config.ts declares parameters with M3LConfigParameter and uses M3LConfigValidators, including an AWS_PROFILE_PARAM_NAME parameter because the script touches AWS
Relative imports carry the .js extension and all exports are named (no default export, no any)
Adds the scripts/s3-export project reference to the root tsconfig.json
Hands off the real logic to implementation rather than writing it inline

evals.prompt: Add a report-generator automation under scripts/ — CLI only, no AWS. It should take a batchSize config parameter.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded scripts/report-generator/ in the modular layout with a batchSize M3LConfigParameter validated by M3LConfigValidators.range, no AWS profile parameter, root tsconfig reference added, built and smoke-run, implementation handed off.
expectations: Creates scripts/report-generator/ with package.json, tsconfig.json, and a modular src/ skeleton (main.ts + config.ts + steps/)
config.ts declares a batchSize parameter with a schema-time validator
Does NOT add an AWS profile parameter since the script is CLI-only with no AWS
main.ts stays a composition root with logic in steps/ modules
Adds the root tsconfig.json project reference for the new package

evals.prompt: Scaffold a new script called s3-export — the batching logic and AWS wiring.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects scripts/s3-export/ already exists and stops, telling the user to implement/edit the existing script directly rather than re-scaffolding it.
expectations: Skill checks whether scripts/s3-export/ already exists before scaffolding
Skill stops and redirects to implementing/editing the existing script when the directory already exists
No files are created or overwritten

evals.prompt: Scaffold a new module for the library — a 'cache' capability. Nothing exists for it yet.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes this is a library module, not a consumer script, and redirects the user to scaffolding-submodules (which scaffolds a new Core/AWS submodule under packages/m3l-common) instead of creating anything under scripts/.
expectations: Skill distinguishes a library module from a consumer script
Skill redirects to scaffolding-submodules for a new library module
Does NOT create anything under scripts/
